channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: India bets ₹10,000 crore on a leap from generic drugs to biologics
item.description: Biopharma SHAKTI aims to build the research, clinical-trial and manufacturing ecosystem India needs to move from low-cost generic copies to high-value biological medicines.

item.title: The mole in the model - you've hired an adversary | origin
item.description: A backdoor in an open-weight model survives fine-tuning and fires on a hidden trigger, so where you run it does not matter. Defense lives on the endpoint.

item.title: Scientists finally solved how a common gut bacterium triggers colon cancer
item.description: Researchers solved a long-standing mystery behind how a bacterial toxin associated with colorectal cancer damages the colon. The toxin first binds to a receptor called claudin-4, giving it access to attack the cells' protective barrier. After identifying…

item.title: Charter — AI agent memory interoperability community group (v1.0)
item.description: Group: AI Agent Memory Interoperability Community GroupGroup home: https://www.w3.org/community/ai-agent-memory-interop/Short name: ai-agent-memory-interopStatus: v1.0 — AdoptedAdopted: 2026-06-19 (call for consensus circulated 2026-06-05; carried absent sust…

item.title: Op-ed: australia vs AI copyright theft and data centres blow by blow
item.description: It’s time for AI to learn to respect property rights. The post Op-Ed: Australia vs AI copyright theft and data centres blow by blow appeared first on Digital Journal.

item.title: A global capital for AI safety is emerging — and it's not in silicon valley
item.description: The United Kingdom is gaining influence through its growing AI-safety ecosystem.

item.title: New research shows that where your sugar comes from may matter more than how much you eat
item.description: New research suggests sugar from whole fruit, dairy, and added sugars affect heart health differently. Here's what the science says about where your sugar comes from.

item.title: Health subcommittee holds hearing to examine ways to drive efficiencies across early-stage clinical development
item.description: The Committee on Energy and Commerce is the oldest standing legislative committee in the U.S. House of Representatives and is vested with the broadest jurisdiction of any congressional authorizing committee.

item.title: Former openai CTO does what altman won't: releases a frontier AI model that's actually open
item.description: Thinking Machines' first open weights model is a 975 billion parameter alternative to Chinese LLMs

item.title: Microsoft is reportedly training salespeople to talk down openai and anthropic | techcrunch
item.description: Microsoft is looking to sell its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective than its competitors' models.

item.title: Applied computing wants to give oil and gas operators an AI model for the entire plant | techcrunch
item.description: Applied Computing has raised a $20M Series A to build a foundation AI model for the oil, gas and petrochemical industry.

item.title: Risk of fracture with gabapentinoid treatment in older adults: a multinational population-based study
item.description: This multinational population-based study examines the association between gabapentinoid treatment and fracture risk in older adults. The research represents a collaborative effort across multiple countries and institutions to evaluate the safety profile of gabapentinoids, commonly prescribed medica

item.title: Efficacy and safety of duvakitug in patients with Crohn's disease (RELIEVE UCCD): a phase 2b, randomised, placebo-controlled trial
item.description: 1. Lancet Gastroenterol Hepatol. 2026 Jul 16:S2468-1253(26)00119-6. doi: 10.1016/S2468-1253(26)00119-6. Online ahead of print. Efficacy and safety of duvakitug in patients with Crohn's disease (RELIEVE UCCD): a phase 2b, randomised, placebo-controlled trial. Jairath V(1), Kierkuś J(2), Duvall GA(3),

item.title: Predicting biotech clinical trials and a new Alzheimer’s drug controversy
item.description: This week on "The Readout LOUD" podcast: a new Alzheimer's drug controversy and prediction markets for clinical trials and FDA drug reviews. Listen now.

item.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about Kalshi bets on clinical trials, a Merck cholesterol pill, and more
item.description: Kalshi, one of the world’s largest prediction market exchanges, plans to take bets on clinical trials and regulatory approvals

item.title: STAT+: FDA approves Merck’s oral PCSK9 drug, a first
item.description: Kalshi allows bets on clinical trial outcomes, Eli Lilly buys a psychedelic medicine, and more biotech news from The Readout

item.title: STAT+: Kalshi comes for biopharma, with prediction markets for clinical trials and FDA approvals
item.description: Prediction markets have permeated the worlds of sports, politics, and reality television shows. Now, the biopharma industry could be next.

item.title: STAT+: Amylyx nears pivotal endocrine drug study readout
item.description: Amylyx Pharmaceuticals will soon read out results from a study evaluating a new treatment for a rare endocrine disorder, a pivotal moment for patients and the company.

item.title: Why is OpenAI selling a ChatGPT basketball?
item.description: You may have heard that OpenAI released its first piece of hardware this week. You may not have heard about the ChatGPT basketball.

item.title: How a former DeepMind researcher raised at a $300M pre-seed valuation before launching a product
item.description: Drawing on more than a decade spent helping build some of the world's most influential AI systems, including research that later informed the development of ChatGPT, Andrew Dai explains why he believes visual AI is one of the next major frontiers in artificial intelligence.

item.title: Moonshot’s upcoming Kimi 3 is expected to close the gap with Anthropic’s Opus 4.8
item.description: The FT reports Kimi K3 will be the largest open AI model from China, with a parameter count between 2 trillion and 3 trillion.

item.title: The Download: OpenAI unveils GPT-Red and heat pumps rise in the US
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring

item.title: The agent evaluation gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a reality-alignment problem, not a coverage problem — and most are shipping to production anyway
item.description: Across 157 enterprises, organizations are granting AI agents more autonomy while trusting the evaluations meant to gate that autonomy less. Half have already shipped an agent that passed their internal evaluations and then failed a customer in production; only one in twenty fully trusts automated ev

item.title: xAI can’t deny Grok makes CSAM anymore. So it’s suing users.
item.description: Elon Musk's xAI files first lawsuit against Grok user accused of making child sex images.

item.title: Precision therapeutics for wound healing: Molecular targets, signalling networks, and advanced drug design strategies
item.description: This review examines precision therapeutic approaches for wound healing, focusing on molecular targets and signalling networks that drive the healing process. The article outlines advanced drug design strategies that leverage these mechanistic insights to develop targeted interventions for wound rep

item.title: Small molecular therapeutic targets for neurodegenerative diseases
item.description: This review examines small molecule therapeutic targets relevant to neurodegenerative diseases, drawing on current research in neurochemistry and biotechnology to identify molecular pathways and compounds of interest for drug development in this therapeutic area.

item.title: Recent strategies and approaches for the development of molecularly targeted nanomedicine
item.description: This review examines current strategies and approaches for developing molecularly targeted nanomedicines, which represent an advancing field in drug delivery technology designed to improve therapeutic specificity and efficacy.

item.title: OpenAI researcher Miles Wang in talks to launch AI drug discovery startup valued at $2B
item.description: The funding discussions point to investor interest in applying AI to make breakthroughs in life sciences.

item.title: High-fidelity fast fluorescence lifetime imaging by event-based denoising
item.description: Event-based first-photon fluorescence lifetime imaging enables fluorescence lifetime imaging below one photon per pixel.

item.title: Early maturation of host antimicrobial peptide defences is associated with host–pathogen coexistence
item.description: Host antimicrobial peptides (AMPs) are a frontline defense against infection. Now, it has been shown that maturation of AMP repertoires early in life can predict stable host coexistence with a highly invasive pathogen.

item.title: Australia wants to ‘manage’ AI. what will that look like?
item.description: Hours before Albanese delivered a major speech on AI in Sydney, the US state of New York imposed a one-year pause on building new data centres.

item.title: Fed’s bowman says tailored AI supervision drives financial inclusion
item.description: Federal Reserve supervision should not hinder banks’ implementation of artificial intelligence (AI), because the technology can contribute to financial inclusion, Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle W. Bowman said Tuesday (July 14). In a speec…

item.title: A bayesian framework for longitudinal EHR and genetic discovery
item.description: A Bayesian generative framework that integrates longitudinal electronic health records with genetic data to identify latent disease signatures is presented.

item.title: Bmw, toyota, bosch and repsol launch real-world pilot of vehicles running on 100% renewable gasoline
item.description: The pilot will provide real-world evidence that renewable fuels can be deployed at scale today using existing vehicles, refuelling infrastructure and digital certification technologies.

item.title: Before the next mythos moment: the case for an AI threat fusion center
item.description: Earlier this spring, Claude’s developers found the cyber potential of Anthropic’s model, Mythos 5, hazardous enough to pump the brakes on model release, creating the Project Glasswing collaboration to give cyber defenders the upper hand through controlled acc…

item.title: An encyclopedia of human enhancer–gene regulatory interactions
item.description: An encyclopedia of more than 92 million enhancer–gene regulatory interactions created as part of the ENCODE4 project provides a valuable resource for future studies of gene regulation and human genetics.

item.title: Profiling the gut resistome to unlock antimicrobial resistance biology and inform clinical risk
item.description: The gut microbiome contains a vast reservoir of antimicrobial resistance genes. In this Review, the authors discuss how gut resistome profiling is advancing understanding of resistance biology while creating new opportunities for surveillance, clinical risk p…

item.title: The star wars cantina scene shows we need a new hope for the agentic web
item.description: 'We don't serve their kind here' protects today's publishers, but won't help rebels destroy the monetization Death Stars built by Google and Meta

item.title: South korea to launch universal basic AI chatbot
item.description: Tender calls for providers to power free service with local LLMs, government to supply some GPUs

item.title: Augmenting Medical Judgment in Signal Confirmation: Design Considerations for an AI-Enabled Causality Assessment Framework
item.description: This paper presents design considerations for an artificial intelligence-enabled framework intended to support medical judgment in causality assessment during drug safety signal confirmation. The framework is developed to augment—rather than replace—human expertise in evaluating potential adverse dr

item.title: Evolution of KRAS molecular glues: from allele-selective to Pan-RAS and protein-protein interaction modulators
item.description: This review examines the development of KRAS-targeted molecular glues, tracing their evolution from allele-selective inhibitors to pan-RAS inhibitors and protein-protein interaction modulators. The article documents advances in targeting strategies for KRAS mutations, a significant focus in oncology

item.title: Primary tumor resection prior to peptide receptor radionuclide therapy is associated with improved survival in metastatic gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors: a systematic review and meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis demonstrates that primary tumor resection performed prior to peptide receptor radionuclide therapy is associated with improved survival outcomes in patients with metastatic gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors. This finding suggests a potential treatment

item.title: FDA Issues Emergency Use Authorization for Drug for Short-Term Prevention of New World Screwworm in Horses
item.description: The FDA today issued an Emergency Use Authorization (EUA) for Ivermectin Liquid for Horses (ivermectin oral solution) for the short-term prevention of New World screwworm (NWS) infestations (myiasis) in horses when administered within 24 hours of birth or at the time of initial wound care.

item.title: STAT+: Swing Therapeutics acquired by medical VR platform XR Health
item.description: Swing Therapeutics, developer of a digital treatment for fibromyalgia, has been acquired by medical virtual reality company XR Health.

item.title: Microsoft is reportedly training salespeople to talk down OpenAI and Anthropic
item.description: Microsoft is looking to sell its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective than its competitors' models.

item.title: Inside Ode with Anthropic, the startup betting AI services are the future of enterprise
item.description: Can a handful of engineers really do the work of an army of consultants? That’s the bet behind Ode with Anthropic — the joint venture dedicated to embedding forward-deployed engineers in enterprise firms, backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, Goldman Sachs

item.title: Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer
item.description: OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring partner to help its other models boost their defenses against cyberattacks. Last week the company released the latest version of its flagship LLM, GPT-5.6. OpenAI says that training it against GPT-Red made the model its m

item.title: Agentic orchestration: Enterprise AI organizations have a deployment problem, not a platform problem — and most are calling chatbots agents
item.description: Across 101 enterprises, agent orchestration is consolidating onto model-provider platforms — Anthropic’s Claude leads by a wide margin — chosen for the gravity of the underlying model and judged on reliable multi-step execution. But the ambition runs well ahead of the reality: most deployed “agents”

item.title: Data centers and energy prices, 5079 disk drive san jose, claude for teachers, more: researchbuzz AI update, july 15, 2026
item.description: DATA CENTERS New York Times: Data Centers to Add Billions in Power Costs in 13 States. This link goes to a gift article. “PJM, the nation’s largest electrical grid operator, on Tuesday releas…

item.title: Spotify founder's health startup neko lands $700m
item.description: Daniel Ek's AI-powered body-scanning clinics expand to US with massive funding round
